Title: LLVM: llvm::PHINode Class Reference
URL: https://llvm.org/doxygen/classllvm_1_1PHINode.html
Content: Public Types | Public Member Functions | Static Public Member Functions | Protected Member Functions | Friends | List of all members. | using | const\_block\_iterator = BasicBlock \* const \* |. | Public Types inherited from llvm::Instruction |. | Public Types inherited from llvm::User |. | using | const\_op\_iterator = const Use\* |. | using | const\_op\_range = iterator\_range<const\_op\_iterator> |. | Public Types inherited from llvm::Value |. | using | const\_user\_iterator = user\_iterator\_impl<const User> |. | const\_block\_iterator | block\_begin () const |. | const\_block\_iterator | block\_end () const |. | iterator\_range< const\_block\_iterator > | blocks () const |. | Value \* | getIncomingValue (unsigned i) const |. | void | setIncomingValue (unsigned i, Value \*V) |. | BasicBlock \* | getIncomingBlock (unsigned i) const |. | BasicBlock \* | getIncomingBlock (const Use &U) const |. | BasicBlock \* | getIncomingBlock (Value::const\_user\_iterator I) const |. | void | setIncomingBlock (unsigned i, BasicBlock \*BB) |. | Value \* | removeIncomingValue (const BasicBlock \*BB, bool DeletePHIIfEmpty=true) |.

Title: [llvm-dev] How to the get the PHI Nodes in a basic block?
URL: https://groups.google.com/g/llvm-dev/c/m1awS5MZOVk
Content: You can use MachineBasicBlock::getFirstNonPHI and then iterate backward. BasicBlock::phis() gives you the range of PHI instructions in that block.

Title: LLVM Concepts — llvmpy 0.9.0 documentation
URL: https://www.llvmpy.org/llvmpy-doc/dev/doc/llvm_concepts.html
Content: This section explains a few concepts related to LLVM, not specific to llvmpy. The intermediate representation, or IR for short, is an in-memory data structure that represents executable code. The IR data structures allow for creation of types, constants, functions, function arguments, instructions, global variables and so on. For example, to create a function *sum* that takes two integers and returns their sum, we need to follow these steps:. * add a *basic block* to the function. * using a helper object called an *instruction builder*, add two instructions into the basic block:. > + an instruction to add the two arguments and store the result into a temporary variable. (A basic block is a block of instructions.). The LLVM demo page lets you type in C or C++ code, converts it into LLVM IR and outputs the IR as LLVM assembly language code. Just to get a feel of the LLVM assembly language, here’s a function in C, and the corresponding LLVM assembly (as generated by the demo page):.

Title: Single-Static Assignment Form and PHI — Mapping High Level Constructs to LLVM IR documentation
URL: https://mapping-high-level-constructs-to-llvm-ir.readthedocs.io/en/latest/control-structures/ssa-phi.html
Content: * Single-Static Assignment Form and PHI. define  i32  @max(i32  %a,  i32  %b)  {entry:  %retval  =  alloca  i32,  align  4  %0  =  icmp  sgt  i32  %a,  %b  br  i1  %0,  label  %btrue,  label  %bfalsebtrue:  ; preds = %2  store  i32  %a,  i32*  %retval,  align  4  br  label  %endbfalse:  ; preds = %2  store  i32  %b,  i32*  %retval,  align  4  br  label  %endend:  ; preds = %btrue, %bfalse  %1  =  load  i32,  i32*  %retval,  align  4  ret  i32  %1}. We can see that the function allocates space on the stack with `alloca` , where the bigger value is stored. In one branch `%a` is stored, while in the other branch `%b` is stored to the stack allocated memory. define  i32  @max(i32  %a,  i32  %b)  {entry:  %0  =  icmp  sgt  i32  %a,  %b  br  i1  %0,  label  %btrue,  label  %bfalsebtrue:  %retval  =  %a  br  label  %endbfalse:  %retval  =  %b  br  label  %endend:  ret  i32  %retval}. end:  %retval  =  phi  i32  [%a,  %btrue],  [%b,  %bfalse].

Title: [PDF] Lecture 6 More on the LLVM Compiler
URL: https://www.cs.cmu.edu/afs/cs/academic/class/15745-s14/public/lectures/L6-LLVM-Part2.pdf
Content: BasicBlock::iterator – Iterates through the instructions in a basic block. Eliminates phi nodes for basic blocks with a single predecessor
